items.acceptance_criteria: An operator runs GITCODE_MCP_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/cache-live gitcode-mcp bind --live --repo-owner "$GITCODE_E2E_OWNER" --repo "$GITCODE_E2E_REPO" and a subsequent gitcode-mcp sync --live reaches the GitCode API rather than fixtures. An operator runs go test ./... with no live env vars set and all tests pass using fixture/offline providers only. An operator runs gitcode-mcp sync (no --live) and the command selects the fixture provider by default.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs gitcode-mcp sync --live with a bound live repo vs go test ./...
items.expected_outcome: sync --live reaches GitCode API; sync without --live uses fixture; go test ./... stays offline and passes
items.freshness: Current binary with --live flag support and live env vars set
items.request_item: Runtime provider-selection wiring for fixture default and live opt-in
items.target_surface: CLI command: gitcode-mcp sync --live, gitcode-mcp bind --live; service.New factory; go test ./...

items.acceptance_criteria: An operator runs gitcode-mcp auth status with GITCODE_TOKEN set and sees a report identifying the token source as "env", with the token value redacted. An operator runs gitcode-mcp auth status with no token set and sees a diagnostic message listing available token source options without revealing secrets. An operator stores a token in the macOS Keychain with a known service/account pair and runs gitcode-mcp auth status --keychain; the report identifies the token source as "keychain" with redacted value. An operator runs GITCODE_TOKEN=invalid gitcode-mcp sync --live and receives a clear auth-failure diagnostic rather than a generic HTTP error.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs gitcode-mcp auth status with and without GITCODE_TOKEN set, and with keychain-stored token
items.expected_outcome: auth status reports token source (env/keychain/none) with redacted value; no-token diagnostics list available sources; invalid token produces clear auth-failure diagnostic
items.freshness: Current binary with auth status command and credential subsystem
items.request_item: Credential handling with env token baseline and optional native Keychain provider
items.target_surface: CLI command: gitcode-mcp auth status; credential pipeline; env var GITCODE_TOKEN; macOS Keychain integration

items.acceptance_criteria: An operator runs gitcode-mcp sync --live against a configured test repo and the cache populates with real issue records (not fixture ISSUE-42), real wiki page records (not WIKI-HOME), comments, and identities sourced from the live API. An operator runs gitcode-mcp sync --live when the GitCode API returns a 429 rate-limit response, and the command reports rate-limit status and exits cleanly rather than crashing. An operator runs gitcode-mcp sync --live after a previous successful sync; the sync event record reflects a delta (or zero delta when nothing changed) and does not duplicate existing records.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs gitcode-mcp sync --live against a configured test repo
items.expected_outcome: Cache populates with real issue records, comments, wiki pages, and identities from live API; rate-limit handled gracefully; re-sync produces delta event without duplicates
items.freshness: Current binary with live sync; live repo and token configured
items.request_item: Live sync behavior for GitCode issues, comments, and wiki pages
items.target_surface: CLI command: gitcode-mcp sync --live; cache database; sync event records

items.acceptance_criteria: An operator runs gitcode-mcp create-issue --live --idempotency-key "ik-001" --title "Test issue" against a test repo; the issue is created on GitCode and a cached record appears with the remote ID. An operator runs the same create-issue command again with the same idempotency key; the command reports "already applied" and does not create a duplicate. An operator runs gitcode-mcp create-issue --live --dry-run --title "Test dry run"; the command validates inputs and shows what would be created without touching the remote API. An operator runs gitcode-mcp create-issue --live --title "Conflicting update" after the remote issue was modified externally; the command detects the conflict and reports it rather than silently overwriting.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs create-issue --live with idempotency key, dry-run, and conflict scenarios
items.expected_outcome: Issue created on GitCode with cached record; duplicate idempotency key reports 'already applied'; dry-run validates without remote call; conflict detected and reported
items.freshness: Current binary with live write support; live repo and token configured
items.request_item: Live write behavior for issues, comments, wiki pages, and labels
items.target_surface: CLI command: gitcode-mcp create-issue --live; gitcode-mcp create-comment --live; gitcode-mcp create-wiki-page --live; audit trail; cache database

items.acceptance_criteria: An operator runs go test -run TestE2ELiveTwoCache -tags=e2e ./internal/e2e/ with GITCODE_E2E_REPO_ID, GITCODE_E2E_OWNER, GITCODE_E2E_REPO, and GITCODE_TOKEN set; the test creates two independent caches from the same live repo and asserts cache content equivalence. The e2e test output and any captured logs contain no private URLs, owner/repo names, tokens, raw Authorization headers, or raw API response bodies. The e2e test cleans up both cache databases on completion regardless of success or failure.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs e2e test with live env vars set
items.expected_outcome: Two independent caches from same live repo contain equivalent data; output contains no private URLs, tokens, or raw API responses; caches cleaned up on completion
items.freshness: Current e2e test binary with live repo and token env vars
items.request_item: Redacted two-cache e2e harness proving remote source of truth
items.target_surface: Go test: go test -run TestE2ELiveTwoCache -tags=e2e ./internal/e2e/; cache databases A and B

items.acceptance_criteria: An operator runs gitcode-mcp sync --index against a fixture-backed cache; sync_status reports zero stale_index entries unless source content actually changed. An operator runs gitcode-mcp index --full against a freshly synced cache; sync_status reports zero stale_index entries. An operator modifies one source and re-runs sync --index; sync_status reports exactly the modified source as stale_index and all others as current.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs sync --index or index --full and checks sync_status
items.expected_outcome: sync_status reports zero stale_index entries after fresh sync/index; reports only modified sources after content change
items.freshness: Current binary with fixed index freshness
items.request_item: Fix stale-index false positives after sync and index
items.target_surface: CLI command: gitcode-mcp sync_status; index engine freshness contract

items.acceptance_criteria: An operator runs gitcode-mcp search_sources "test" after fixture sync/index and receives non-empty results instead of cache_empty. An operator runs gitcode-mcp search_sources "notfound" and receives an empty result set (not an error) when no source matches. An MCP client invokes the search_sources tool after sync and receives source records matching the query.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs search_sources after sync/index; MCP client invokes search_sources tool
items.expected_outcome: search_sources returns non-empty results for matching queries; returns empty set for non-matching queries; no cache_empty error
items.freshness: Current binary with fixed search_sources; synced cache
items.request_item: Fix source search so search_sources works after sync/index
items.target_surface: CLI command: gitcode-mcp search_sources; MCP tool: search_sources

items.acceptance_criteria: An MCP client queries list_sources tool schema and sees issue and wiki in the kind enum. An MCP client queries search_sources tool schema and sees issue and wiki in the kind filter enum. An MCP client queries search_chunks tool schema and sees issue and wiki in the kind filter enum. Running gitcode-mcp --mcp and connecting an MCP inspector shows the corrected schemas.
items.actor_trigger: MCP client queries tool schemas; operator runs gitcode-mcp --mcp and inspects with MCP inspector
items.expected_outcome: Kind enums include issue and wiki; legacy enums replaced or supplemented
items.request_item: Fix MCP tool schemas and docs for real source kind enums
items.target_surface: MCP tool schemas for list_sources, search_sources, search_chunks; MCP inspector

items.acceptance_criteria: Running gitcode-mcp --help lists all registered subcommands and exits 0. Running gitcode-mcp sync --help prints help text and exits 0. Running gitcode-mcp index --help prints help text and exits 0. Running gitcode-mcp bind --help prints help text and exits 0. Running gitcode-mcp auth --help prints help text and exits 0. No subcommand --help path returns an invalid-query diagnostic.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs --help on root and every registered subcommand
items.expected_outcome: All subcommands return valid help text, exit 0, no invalid-query diagnostics
items.request_item: Fix CLI help and command discovery for valid subcommands

items.acceptance_criteria: An operator opens an iteration 2 cache database with iteration 3 gitcode-mcp; the tool reports schema version mismatch, the detected version, the expected version, and an actionable message (re-init or migrate). An operator runs gitcode-mcp migrate-cache (or equivalent) against an old cache; the schema is upgraded in place without data loss for schemas that are migration-compatible. An operator opens an iteration 1 cache database with iteration 3; the tool reports incompatibility and recommends re-initialization.
items.actor_trigger: Operator opens an iteration 1 or 2 cache database with iteration 3 binary; operator runs migrate-cache against old cache
items.expected_outcome: Schema version mismatch reported with actionable message; migration upgrades schema in place without data loss for compatible schemas; iteration 1 cache reports incompatibility
items.freshness: Current binary; iteration 1 and 2 cache database files
items.request_item: Cache schema compatibility and migration for old databases
items.target_surface: CLI command: gitcode-mcp (implicit on open); gitcode-mcp migrate-cache; cache database

items.acceptance_criteria: An operator runs gitcode-mcp doctor against a configured live setup; the output includes version, config, cache, repo binding, token source (redacted), live provider reachable, auth probe pass/fail, last sync time, index freshness, and MCP transport status — all with no private coordinates or tokens exposed. An operator runs gitcode-mcp doctor with no repo binding configured; the output reports "no repo bound" and suggests the bind command. An operator runs gitcode-mcp doctor with a bound repo but no token; the output reports "no token configured" and lists available token sources.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs gitcode-mcp doctor with various configurations (live, no-binding, no-token)
items.expected_outcome: Doctor reports version, config, cache, repo binding, token source (redacted), live provider, auth probe, last sync, index freshness, MCP transport — all public-safe; no-binding reports 'no repo bound'; no-token reports available sources
items.request_item: Doctor/readiness output for operator self-service setup

items.acceptance_criteria: An MCP client connects to gitcode-mcp --mcp over stdio after live sync and invokes list_sources; the response includes real issue and wiki source records from the live repo. An MCP client connects over HTTP/SSE transport and invokes search_sources with a query matching live data; the response returns correct results. The local macOS Keychain wrapper launches gitcode-mcp --mcp and an MCP client successfully invokes all read tools against the live-synced cache. A test script validates that all MCP read tools return non-error responses against a freshly synced live cache.
items.actor_trigger: MCP client connects to gitcode-mcp --mcp after live sync and invokes all read tools
items.expected_outcome: All MCP read tools return correct results against live-synced cache; HTTP/SSE and stdio wrapper paths work
items.request_item: MCP parity validation for connected-client reads with live cache
items.target_surface: MCP server: gitcode-mcp --mcp over stdio and HTTP/SSE; all MCP read tools

items.acceptance_criteria: A new operator reading docs/live-readiness.md can configure a repo binding, supply a token, run live sync, perform a live write, and validate the cache — all using sanitized placeholders and env var references. docs/architecture.md includes the provider-selection decision tree and credential flow. README.md or a dedicated public-safety doc lists the sanitization rules for live transcripts and fixtures. docs/cache-and-sync-model.md documents live sync event semantics, partial failure behavior, and cache migration policy.
items.actor_trigger: New operator reads docs/live-readiness.md and follows setup guide; maintainer reviews architecture.md
items.expected_outcome: Operator can configure repo, supply token, run live sync, perform live write, and validate cache from docs alone; architecture.md includes provider-selection and credential flow; public-safety rules documented; live sync semantics documented
items.freshness: Current docs in repository after iteration 3 implementation
items.target_surface: docs/architecture.md, docs/live-readiness.md, docs/cache-and-sync-model.md, README.md, MCP tool schema docs
